docs.title: Welcome to fruit.
docs.text:
A large and extensive hub for all designer and developer needs. Everything here is 100% free. You can help us by sending Pull Requests to add more services or by removing ones whose offerings have changed or been retired.

Please do note if you find an issue with any service listed here, report it to them, not us! (we didn't make it, we are only listing it!)

Fruit is still being updated. We are aware that all the categories are not filled. This project is inspired by free-for.dev! Check them out!

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: The file where services are listed at is docs.md inside the ./docs folder. That is the file you edit. The service should have a free tier that is safe to use and does not risk security. The service can be your own or from someone you own, and you may list it as long as it is having a reasonable uptime.

docs.title: When adding items to it, please keep these in mind:
docs.text:
The service must be listed under the category where it fits most.

It must list an icon, of the service. Use this resource when finding one. If no icon is avaliable for it through there, keep a similar icon with it, for instance if Gmail's icon is not there, you can use: Google (Material) :material-google: Google (Fontawesome) :fontawesome-brands-google: Google (Simple) :simple-google:

The description for the service either has to be a small description of an official page of theirs or a small description. You also should signify what platforms the services are avalable in with the icons: + . Used if both Intel and M1 and above series of chips are supported. + Used if only M1 and above series of chips are supported.

When uploading it to GitHub as a Pull Request, please do not upload the built version, and instead just upload the modified docs.md file

docs.title: You must format it like how it is below:
docs.text: Fruit - A large and extensive hub for all designer and developer needs. Everything here is 100% free.

docs.title: How do I preview the page, build it or change functionality of the page?
docs.text:
You will need Python 3.4 or Higher which you can get here. Run pip install mkdocs-material (the mkdocs skin we use, should come with mkdocs as a presquity) and then check if mkdocs is installed with mkdocs --version. If not, run pip install mkdocs. You can also run pip install -r requirements.txt alternatively. Then clone this repository using git clone https://github.com/hamhimdev/fruit. You can now edit index.md in the docs folder as much as you want following the pull request guidelines before this topic. After you are done, you need to be in the parent folder of the docs folder, and if you have changed anything, such as the theme, or need additonal features, add it through mkdocs.yml. You can refer to the mkdocs page and squidfunk's material theme for mkdocs if you need to. To preview it, run mkdocs serve and follow the URL (Likely http://127.0.0.1:8000/) shown in the output and open it in your browser. It should show you how it looks. When in the parent foler, run mkdocs build and it will build the (static) page of the markdown file. More details can be found at the mkdocs page and squidfunk's material theme for mkdocs with additional settings and features even we didn't use in fruit!

This section is getting a rework, please follow the steps above.

docs.title: Windows
docs.text: To do this, please download Python 3.4 or Higher here.

docs.title: Services/Software
docs.text:
• We mark what Operating Systems and platforms these Services/Software supports with .
  • When we mention +, it means it only supports the M series chips of Apple's Computers.

docs.title: Categories
docs.text:
• Communication services
  • Email services
  • IDEs
  • Vector/Image manipulation software
  • Operating systems
  • Icon libraries
  • Font libraries
  • Office suites
  • Documentation solutions
  • Search engines

docs.title: Communication services
docs.text:
• Discord - A place that makes it easy to talk every day and hang out more often.
  • Guilded - Guilded upgrades your group chat and equips your server with integrated event calendars, forums, and more – 100% free.
  • Skype - A popular platform in it's old days. Now owned by Microsoft.
  • TeamSpeak - Use crystal clear sound to communicate with your team mates cross-platform with military-grade security, lag-free performance & unparalleled reliability and uptime.
  • Slack - Connect the right people, find anything you need and automate the rest. That’s work in Slack, your productivity platform.
  • REVOLT - Revolt is one of the best ways to stay connected with your friends and community without sacrificing any usability. We focus only on the user, and you can be sure that your conversations are confidential and your data is secure.

docs.title: Email services
docs.text:
• Fastmail - Fast, private email that’s just for you.
  • Gmail - Gmail is email that's intuitive, efficient, and useful.
  • Outlook - Outlook is a free, personal email service.
  • Proton Mail - Keep your conversations private. Proton Mail is an encrypted email service based in Switzerland.
  • Temp Mail - Protect your personal email address from spam, bots, phishing and other online abuse. Temporary anonymous email address - no commitments and no risks.
  • yahoo!mail - Get stuff done, with a whopping 1TB of free storage, and utterlessly useful features!
  • Zoho Mail - Host your business email on a secure, encrypted, privacy-guaranteed, and ad-free email service.

docs.title: IDEs
docs.text:
• CodeBlocks - Code::Blocks is a free C/C++ and Fortran IDE built to meet the most demanding needs of its users. It is designed to be very extensible and fully configurable.
  • Visual Studio Code - Free code editor. Built on open source. Runs everywhere.

docs.title: Vector/Image manipulation software
docs.text:
• Figma - Figma connects everyone in the design process so teams can deliver better products, faster.
  • Lunacy - Free design software that keeps your flow with AI tools and built-in graphics.
